public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as quartermaster.
appearance: Korin Drillwake carries the marks of quartermaster in industrial grit: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Treats exchange as a social instrument as much as an economic one, reading appetite and hesitation alike. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a quartermaster still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of quartermaster in and around Soreheim Proper, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Soreheim Proper while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to work shortfalls is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Korin Drillwake can entangle the party in price, debt, or delivery terms that become more dangerous than they first appear.
Korin Drillwake will trade route, scarcity, or faction insight once the party resolves a commercial problem others cannot touch openly.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Textiles, tokens, tally habits, and practiced bargaining poise make Korin Drillwake read as someone who lives by exchange and reputation. In Soreheim Proper, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Giant Council, Cloud Giant governance cadres.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Soreheim Proper: markets, counting tables, depots, ports, inns, or contract rooms
who_avoids_them: those who waste time without value

first_impression: Korin Drillwake presents as Treats exchange as a social instrument as much as an economic one, reading appetite and hesitation alike. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a quartermaster still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Quartermaster, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Soreheim Alliance.
what_they_refuse: Korin Drillwake refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Quartermaster too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Korin Drillwake can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Korin Drillwake adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Korin Drillwake is Known locally through day-to-day work as quartermaster. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Quartermaster.
social_texture: Korin Drillwake speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Soreheim Proper, people read them as quota-aware, contribution-minded, and never fully off the clock.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to production; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but work shortfalls becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Korin touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Korin Drillwake when the party needs a socially grounded face for Soreheim Proper: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
